# UC-12.7.8 · Ansible AWX Inventory Sync Failure and Host Unreachable Trending

## Description

Stale inventories cause automation to target the wrong hosts or skip new systems, while rising unreachable counts flag VPN, firewall, or credential regressions across fleets. Combining AWX inventory job status with play outcomes surfaces controller-side versus host-side problems.

## Value

Protects patch coverage and compliance scans from silent shrinkage when cloud or CMDB feeds break.

## Implementation

Replace `baseline_unreach` with a subsearch or `eventstats` median over 7d. Ensure `inventory_name` and `organization` exist on AWX events. For `ansible:result`, aggregate unreachable per hour per org.

## SPL

```spl
index=iac (sourcetype="ansible:awx" OR sourcetype="ansible:result")
| eval inv_fail=if(sourcetype="ansible:awx" AND match(lower(status),"(?i)failed|error"),1,0)
| eval unreachable_evt=if(sourcetype="ansible:result" AND unreachable>0,1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(inv_fail) as inventory_sync_failures, sum(unreachable_evt) as unreachable_runs, dc(inventory_name) as inventories_touched by _time, organization
| eventstats median(unreachable_runs) as med_unreach by organization
| where inventory_sync_failures > 0 OR unreachable_runs > med_unreach * 3
| sort -inventory_sync_failures
```

## Visualization

Dual-metric timechart, table (inventory × last error), map or breakdown by cloud account if available.

## References

- [AWX — Inventory import](https://ansible.readthedocs.io/projects/awx/en/latest/inventory/index.html)
